text: <par><latinform>kha</latinform> = relationship to <latinform>KA</latinform> 181/229 gall, bile, liver? other E=HEART/LIVER?</par>
<par>M2= or 1319 or 1320? M3: cf. <latinform>p/bra</latinform>? (above and p. 28)</par>
<par>Perhaps allofamically related is the first syllable of Lalung <latinform>phidi</latinform> 'liver'.</par>
<par>Cf. also Lalung <latinform>kur</latinform> 'skin', pointing to a possible alternation between Lalung final <latinform>-n</latinform> and <latinform>-r</latinform>.</par>

<par>A Lalung subtlety should be noted: the first elements of <latinform>tu-dar</latinform> 'penis' and <latinform>tu ki ku-thi</latinform> 'testicle' are to be assigned to <xref ref="3420">#3420</xref>; on the other hand, the first element of Lalung <latinform>tu-di</latinform> 'egg' is from <xref ref="1604">#1604</xref> 'bird'. This is somewhat confusing, since <xref ref="300">#300</xref> sometimes means 'testicle' rather than 'egg'.</par>

<par>Lalung <latinform>tu-</latinform> here means "bird" (< <xref ref="1604">#1604</xref>). But in the Lalung compound <latinform>tu-dar</latinform> 'penis', Lalung <latinform>tu-</latinform> is assigned to <xref ref="3420">#3420</xref>.</par>

<par>The <latinform>-b</latinform> in <latinform>-jub-</latinform> represents an unreleased (not truly voiced) stop. Lalung has a doublet <latinform>chu-ma</latinform> 'kiss', from the open-syllable allofam <xref ref="5539">#5539</xref>, below.</par>
<par>Lalung <latinform>-b</latinform> represents an unreleased (not truly voiced) stop.</par>
